Hi all,

Smartphones are a little computers which are possible to put to the pocket.
Their performance increasing year by year.

Today, I want write about the Termux app on Samsung Galaxy S7 edge** smartphone.

In Google Play Store absent The Termux app, so I installed F-Droid app and next old Termux version.

When I launched Termux it is already connected to the internet using Galaxy S7 edge connections.

I checked IP address using ifconfig command.

To update repositories and packages I used pkg update command.

To be able to establish ssh connection from Macbook I installed openssh package.
To start ssh deamon I run sshd command.
I checked current user using whoami command.

On Macbook I run ssh <user>@<ipaddress>command.
A little help I found on Muhammad Ibnuh’s blog.

To be able to install usufull console apps I add root-repo:

$ pkg install root-repo

To be able to monitor smartphone perfomance I will use htop app:

$ pkg install htop

To be able to clone repositories git app is needed:

$ pkg install git

Run htop app just like that is not possible - I need root privileges.

$ pkg install tsu

The idea of application to install I found in Jan C. Borchardt account, in nextcloud-scripts repository.

I run the following commands:

$ pkg install php

$ pkg install php-fpm

$ pkg install sqlite

$ pkg install coreutils

$ pkg install openssl-tool

$ termux-setup-storage

$ cd /storage/emulated/0/

$ git clone https://github.com/nextcloud/server.git nextcloud

$ cd /storage/emulated/0/nextcloud/ && git submodule update --init

It is everything that I have for today.
Everyone is a blacksmith of own fate.
Mateusz.
